summary: Converted the ftgigakoopa character unit from a standalone assembly object into a mostly C-matched source/header pair, while leaving ftGKoopa_OnDeath as inline asm because its f32 accesses conflicted with then-current Fighter field typing. The PR also adjusted build lists to compile src/melee/ft/chara/ftgigakoopa.o, declared lbl_803D3984 as a const s32 array, refined Fighter flag layout around offsets 0x2229-0x222B, and added commented decomp.me-derived candidate matches for small Kirby and Luigi helper functions.

key_files.role: Deleted 288-line assembly source after replacement by C/inline-asm unit.
New matched C file for ftgigakoopa functions; keeps ftGKoopa_OnDeath as inline asm and implements wrappers, item checks, flag writes, slope/scale helpers, and table lookup logic.
New header declaring ftGKoopa_OnDeath, ftGKoopa_OnLoad, and unnamed ftgigakoopa helper symbols.
Adjusted Fighter layout to expose x222A_flag between x2229 and x222B padding.
Changed lbl_803D3984 from unknown commented extern to extern const s32 lbl_803D3984[].
Switched ftgigakoopa object from asm path to src path.
Added commented C candidate and decomp.me scratch link for func_800EEBC0.
Added commented C candidate and decomp.me scratch link for func_8014260C.

what_changed: Removed asm/melee/ft/chara/ftgigakoopa.s and added src/melee/ft/chara/ftgigakoopa.c with 141 lines of source.

Matched most ftgigakoopa functions in C, including simple forwarding wrappers around func_80132A64 and func_80132D00, OnLoad setup, held-item state checks, calls to func_80070FB4/func_80070C48/func_80070CC4, two func_800704F0 helpers using 3.0f and 0.0f, and small action-index helper functions func_8014F98C and func_8014F9A4.
Kept ftGKoopa_OnDeath as inline asm in the new C file rather than fully converting it.
Added #pragma peephole on after the inline asm block, indicating compiler-codegen matching control was part of the tactic.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftgigakoopa.h with prototypes and includes for HSD_GObj and Fighter.
Updated obj_files.mk so ftgigakoopa builds from src/melee/ft/chara/ftgigakoopa.o instead of asm/melee/ft/chara/ftgigakoopa.o.
Added ftgigakoopa.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk.
Declared lbl_803D3984 as extern const s32 lbl_803D3984[] in include/variables.h for the table lookup in func_8014F9A4.
Changed Fighter layout near offset 0x222A by replacing a 3-byte filler with filler_x2229, x222A_flag, and filler_x222B so ftGKoopa_OnLoad could set ft->x222A_flag.bits.b0.
Added commented C snippets and scratch links for func_800EEBC0 in ftkirby.s and func_8014260C in ftluigi.s, described by the PR author as extra matching functions left in comments.
The author rebased the PR to fix conflicts before merge.
smart_moves: Separated the matchable low-risk functions from the hard struct-layout problem by converting most of the file while preserving ftGKoopa_OnDeath as inline asm.
Made the build-system transition explicit by swapping the object path in obj_files.mk and adding the source object to e_files.mk.
Introduced only the specific Fighter field exposure needed for x222A_flag rather than attempting a broad Fighter refactor in this PR.
Used a typed global declaration, extern const s32 lbl_803D3984[], to support C table access instead of keeping the label unknown.
Preserved useful future work in comments for Kirby/Luigi helper functions with decomp.me scratch URLs, even though those functions were not converted in this PR.
Called out UB risk directly in source for func_8014F9A4: the offset variable may be uninitialized unless arg0 is 0xF.
decomp_lessons: It is acceptable and productive to land a mostly matched source file while leaving one difficult routine as inline asm when the blocker is a larger struct-typing issue.

Fighter field typing matters for matching: ftGKoopa_OnDeath performs f32 loads/stores to offsets currently represented as scalar-only fields, suggesting that premature field names/types can block clean C.
When an offset is used as flags, exposing the exact byte as an UnkFlagStruct can enable bitfield writes like ft->x222A_flag.bits.b0 without disturbing adjacent layout.
Table lookups against data labels should be backed by appropriately typed extern declarations in include/variables.h.
Small action-remapping functions can sometimes be matched with intentionally narrow switch/if logic, even when it creates apparent uninitialized-variable behavior that mirrors original codegen.
Leaving decomp.me scratch-derived C as comments in still-asm files can preserve matching knowledge for later conversion, but it should be treated as provisional until compiled into C.
naming_conventions: Gigakoopa exported lifecycle functions use the prefix ftGKoopa_, as in ftGKoopa_OnDeath and ftGKoopa_OnLoad.

Unidentified local helpers retained address-based names such as func_8014F698, func_8014F720, func_8014F800, func_8014F848, func_8014F890, func_8014F8E4, func_8014F904, func_8014F948, func_8014F98C, and func_8014F9A4.
Fighter struct fields in this era use offset-prefixed names such as x10C_ftData, x1974_heldItem, x2226_flag, and x222A_flag.
Unknown data labels retain lbl_ names but can be typed when evidence supports it, as with lbl_803D3984 declared const s32[].
Header guard style used in the new file is _ftgigakoopa_h_.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used inline asm with nofralloc for ftGKoopa_OnDeath inside the new C file to avoid blocking the entire translation unit on one nontrivial function.
Enabled peephole optimization after the inline asm block with #pragma peephole on.
Matched simple branch/wrapper functions directly as one-call C wrappers, e.g. func_8014F698 calling func_80132A64 and func_8014F8E4 calling func_80132D00.
Matched repeated held-item guards using func_8026B2B4(ft->x1974_heldItem) == 0 before calls into fighter item/state helper functions.
Used a switch on func_8026B320(ft->x1974_heldItem) to map cases 1, 2, 3, and 4 onto func_80070FB4 arguments 1, 0, 2, and 3 respectively.
For func_8014F9A4, used a deliberately sparse switch that may leave offset uninitialized, matching the original assembly pattern and annotated with a UB warning.

review_feedback.author: RevoSucks
review_feedback.comment: Asked why ftGKoopa_OnDeath was not converted too: "Why not do this one?"

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Explained that ftGKoopa_OnDeath was the only nontrivial function and likely required rethinking Fighter internals because it does f32 operations on values currently represented as scalar-only fields; suggested it might need a union or FighterHeader idiom and deferred to maintainers with more experience.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Noted the branch was rebased to fix conflicts.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully convert ftGKoopa_OnDeath or refactor Fighter fields around x18B0, x222C, and x2230 to support f32 access.
Search for later uses of FighterHeader idiom or unions in Fighter internals after this review discussion.
Check whether func_800EEBC0 in ftkirby.s and func_8014260C in ftluigi.s were later moved from commented C into compiled source.
Search for PRs touching x222A_flag or x2226_flag to confirm the final Fighter bitfield layout.
Look for later cleanup of func_8014F9A4's UB warning or renaming of lbl_803D3984.
